assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
A dividend has been declared within the group and a corresponding intragroup receivable has been recognised before consolidation eliminations.
The issue raised is the accounting relevance of hedge designation for foreign exchange risk on that intragroup dividend-related amount.

approaches.label: IFRS 9 hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The question directly asks whether foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend-related amount can be documented in a hedge relationship, which is governed by IFRS 9 hedge accounting requirements.

approaches.label: Consolidation elimination
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 requires full elimination of intragroup assets, income, expenses and cash flows, making consolidation accounting a distinct top-level model relevant to an intragroup dividend receivable and dividend income.

approaches.label: Separate financial statements accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 27 provides a distinct accounting model under which dividends from a subsidiary are recognised when the right to receive the dividend is established, which is relevant because the fact pattern refers to recognition of a corresponding receivable.
